'''Lon Wert''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Vulptereen/Legends|Vulptereen]] from the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]'s planet of [[Vulpter/Legends|Vulpter]]. At the time of the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Lon Wert was a talented [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], and among the people of his homeworld he had earned a reputation as the most skilled [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|racer]] since [[Dud Bolt/Legends|Dud Bolt]]. He wanted to see his people rebuild their livelihoods, and for that reason he joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].

==Biography==
Among the capable [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] on all of planet [[Vulpter/Legends|Vulpter]], Lon Wert was known not only for his daredevil flying skills but also for his unique understanding of how vehicles worked, which helped him earn a name for himself as the most talented racer since [[Dud Bolt/Legends|Dud Bolt]],<ref name="WC" /> the famous professional [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer]] pilot during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="DB">{{DB|character|dudbolt|Bolt, Dud}}</ref>

When the galaxy was under the rule of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Lon Wert had more sympathy for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]] movement because he believed that the Empire's overthrow would help his people rebuild their livelihoods. Wert dreamed of joining the Rebel Alliance as a fighter pilot, but he remained on Vulpter to help support the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|war]] effort there.<ref name="WC" />

Unfortunately, Wert's arrogance had led him to accumulate debts through [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]]. As a result, he found himself deeply in debt to a local [[Crime lord/Legends|crimelord]]. At that very moment, the annual race, the [[Vulpter Orbital]], was taking place, and Lon Wert was one of the contestants. The crimelord wanted to ensure that Wert did not survive the race as a warning to anyone who thought they could avoid paying what they owed. The Alliance, however, did not want to see their contact harmed and sent a party to Vulpter with a dual mission. Not only did the group have to ensure that Wert survived the race and eliminate any of the crimelord's [[Thug/Legends|thugs]] who tried to sabotage him, but they also had to place a significant wager on Wert's victory and make certain it came to pass in order to pay off the Vulptereen's debt in full.<ref name="WC" />

==Personality and abilities==
[[File:Dud_Bolt.jpg|left|thumb|The skills of Lon Wert were not inferior in professionalism to those of the famous racer Dud Bolt]]
Lon Wert was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Vulptereen/Legends|Vulptereen]] [[Tech specialist|tech specialist]] and [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]. Considered one of the most talented pilots on his homeworld, he was a young and impulsive individual with a tendency toward bragging. Known for his daredevil flying skills and his strong understanding of vehicle mechanics, Wert earned a reputation as a highly impressive [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|racer]], though his arrogance also led him to accumulate [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] debts.<ref name="WC" />

In addition to his piloting abilities, Wert was trained as a mechanic and specialized in [[Starship/Legends|starship]] technology, with particular expertise in crafting and repairing spacecraft. He was proficient in starfighter operation and trained in the use of [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]] and simple weapons, favoring a blaster pistol at range while relying on his natural bite in close combat. He spoke and read [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] and [[Vulpterish]], and his standard equipment consisted of a blaster pistol, a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], a pilot's uniform, and a tool kit.<ref name="WC" />

==Behind the scenes==
A character summary of Lon Wert was presented by [[Wizards of the Coast]] on the [[Wizards.com|official website]] in a supplementary article dedicated to the planet Vulpter for their [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|own roleplaying game]] in [[2005]]. This non-player character served as a key element in the adventure scenario «Racing in the Rings», in which the players took on the roles of members of the Rebel Alliance. The players were tasked with ensuring that Lon Wert, acting as a contact for the organization, was not harmed by a local crimelord during the annual race, as the crimelord sought to ensure that Wert did not survive the competition.
